description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three wide, black metal hoops. The wood has a rich, medium-brown color with visible grain and subtle surface imperfections, giving it a rustic and authentic appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top being fully closed and flush with the rim. The black metal bands are evenly spaced, providing structural reinforcement and a visual contrast to the wood. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface finish is slightly glossy, suggesting a protective coating. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as streets, parks, or near storefronts, often used for storage, decoration, or as a makeshift table.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with three black metal hoops, flat closed top, and a slightly glossy finish. It has a rustic look and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slight angle, showing the flat wooden top and the curvature of the staves with three black metal hoops.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side of the barrel, with the wood grain and metal bands clearly visible and some minor surface imperfections.
description_view_2: The barrel is shown from another side, emphasizing the even spacing of the metal hoops and the smooth, uninterrupted wood surface.
description_view_3: This view closely resembles view 1, with the wood texture and metal bands visible, and a few small dents or marks on the wood.
